title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges France in trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner following their near-perfect UEFA qualification campaign and Euro 2024 title, while France surged to the top of April's FIFA rankings on dominant recent form, keeping probabilities tightly bunched at 17.2% and 16.4%. England's 11.1% reflects solid but unproven major tournament pedigree, with Argentina (8.8%) and Brazil (8.6%) close behind despite CONMEBOL's grueling qualifiers. The expanded 48-team format amplifies chaos—32 knockout advancement spots, up to eight matches in 39 days testing squad depth and stamina amid U.S. summer heat (WBGT often >28°C), cross-continental travel, and altitude edges for highland nations like Ecuador or Colombia, leveling the field for upsets among the elite contenders.

title: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect on April 17, following historic direct talks in Washington—the first since 1993—aimed at halting the Israel-Hezbollah war that erupted on March 2 amid Hezbollah's attacks supporting Iran. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun agreed to the pause to facilitate long-term negotiations, though Israel plans to retain troops south of the Litani River for security, drawing Hezbollah opposition and immediate rocket fire into northern Israel alongside Israeli strikes. Fragility persists with skirmishes reported post-announcement, as traders assess prospects for extension before the truce expires around April 27, potentially ahead of a proposed Netanyahu-Aoun White House summit.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 44.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA Championship after clinching the Western Conference's No. 1 seed with the league's best 64-18 regular-season record, positioning them for a favorable playoff path starting April 18 against a play-in winner. The San Antonio Spurs follow at 15% following their breakout 62-20 campaign and No. 2 West seed versus Portland, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominance and a franchise-record start. Boston Celtics hold 12.2% as East No. 2 seeds (56-26) facing Philadelphia, leveraging playoff experience despite a competitive conference led by Detroit's 60-22 top mark. Denver Nuggets' 9.5% reflects their steady 54-28 third seed versus Minnesota, with recent play-in resolutions solidifying brackets amid clean injury reports for frontrunners.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will the Fed increase interest rates by 25+ bps after the April 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 99.2% implied probability of no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting steady economic data and Federal Reserve patience following the March 17-18 hold at 3.5%-3.75%. March nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs with unemployment at 4.3%, while recent initial jobless claims declined further, signaling labor market resilience amid elevated but stable inflation per March CPI. FOMC minutes released April 8 underscored one potential 2026 rate cut later, with openness to hikes only on shocks like oil disruptions. Upside risks include hotter-than-expected April CPI or retail sales; downside could emerge from weakening employment, though current trader consensus dismisses near-term shifts.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors California Governor Gavin Newsom at 28% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by his sustained national fundraising edge, executive experience, and a March California primary poll showing him leading Kamala Harris by 14 points in their home state amid a wide-open post-2024 field. Recent developments at the April 10-11 National Action Network convention in New York highlighted Black voter courtship, with Harris drawing the loudest cheers and signaling she's "thinking about" another bid, yet traders discount her due to 2024 baggage and uneven national polling. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez holds second at 8% on youth and progressive appeal, while governors like Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results, early primary state polls, DNC calendar fights, and factional endorsements from moderates versus the party's left wing.

title: What will be said during the PGL Bucharest 2026 Grand Final?
description:
The PGL Bucharest 2026 Grand Final is scheduled for April 11, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the official English broadcast of the PGL Bucharest 2026 Grand Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Mentions of the term originating from in-game audio—even in-game characters, the in-game announcer, or player voice comms—will also count toward this market's resolution.

If prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video WILL count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

If this event is definitively cancelled or otherwise not aired by April 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the official English livestream of the PGL Bucharest 2026 Grand Final at https://www.youtube.com/@pgl.

eventMetadata.context_description: FUT Esports claimed their first Tier 1 CS2 trophy with a dominant 3-1 BO5 grand final win over Astralis at PGL Bucharest 2026 on April 11, sweeping Ancient and Mirage 13-5 each, dropping a tight Nuke 14-16, and crushing Dust2 16-3. This marathon series across four maps amplified caster mentions of core terms like pistol rounds (one per map), bomb plants and defuses, and smokes in utility-heavy strats from stars like lauNX and HooXi. Commentary by Anders Blume, HenryG, and moses emphasized FUT's aggression versus Astralis' CT resilience, with trader consensus reflecting VOD review for official English broadcast counts, including in-game audio—high frequency for staples like "bomb" amid no major roster or patch shifts pre-event.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner at 19%, just ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 17%, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio surging to 11% amid a closely contested field reflecting the Republican Party's strong bench. The late-March CPAC straw poll, where Vance captured 53% and Rubio 35% of GOP respondents for the nomination, has anchored Republican frontrunners by signaling Trump-era continuity and Rubio's foreign policy gains in Venezuela and Iran. Newsom leads Democratic nominee odds but faces California governance critiques. This tight race persists due to open primaries, uncertain post-midterm dynamics in swing states, and pending endorsements; 2026 midterm results, scandals, or Trump signals could widen gaps before the November 2028 Electoral College vote.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran direct talks mediated by Pakistan in Islamabad concluded without a permanent peace deal after 21 hours of negotiations around April 12, prompting the US to impose a naval blockade on Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz, while a fragile ceasefire holds. Recent diplomatic backchannels, including Pakistani meetings in Tehran, signal progress toward a framework agreement addressing nuclear enrichment curbs, missile limits, sanctions relief, and Hormuz access, with a second round of discussions potentially this week per US officials. Trader consensus reflects persistent gaps between US demands for zero uranium enrichment and site dismantlement versus Iran's calls for full sanctions lift and reparations, amid heightened escalation risks from military posturing. Upcoming talks and UN mediation efforts could shift dynamics before key deadlines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. commands 49% trader consensus for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, driven by his surging popularity from MAHA policy wins like school lunch reforms and vaccine scrutiny, plus his cousin's March declaration of a definite run and strong early polling. Vice President J.D. Vance trails at 38.5% as the structural frontrunner with incumbency advantages, while Secretary of State Marco Rubio's odds climbed to 21.6% after donors overwhelmingly cheered him over Vance in a recent Trump meeting. Absent a Trump endorsement and with midterms looming, the open primary reflects skin-in-the-game bets on cabinet stars amid fluid GOP succession dynamics.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen lead as the clear frontrunner with "Liekinheitin" at 37.4% implied probability on Polymarket, following their dominant UMK national final victory in late February and subsequent surge in betting odds across bookmakers, fueled by the duo's classical violin-pop fusion praised as a potential stage monster. France's Monroe ("Regarde!") holds second at 11.3% amid strong Big Five auto-qualification buzz and jury appeal, while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard ("Før vi går hjem") climbed to 10.5% on recent OGAE poll momentum and fan voting traction. With all 35 entries released for the Vienna contest (May 12-16, hosted by 2025 winners Austria), trader consensus reflects post-national final hype, though rehearsals and previews could spark shifts in this closely watched field.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' commanding start to the 2026 F1 season under new power unit and active aerodynamics regulations has positioned George Russell and Kimi Antonelli as overwhelming trader favorites for the Drivers' Championship, with the teammates occupying the top two spots in standings after three rounds—Antonelli leading on 72 points with wins in China and Japan, Russell close behind at 63 following victory in Australia. Their intra-team battle reflects Mercedes' front-row lockouts and podium sweeps, while Ferrari's Charles Leclerc trails at 49 points amid signs of closing the gap per team principal Toto Wolff's recent comments. Russell's experience edges his implied probability ahead of the rookie sensation, as traders weigh sustained pace against potential reliability or strategy shifts in the 21 remaining Grands Prix.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs Bilibili Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 16 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Bilibili Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Bilibili Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Top Esports and Bilibili Gaming. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TES (-1.5) vs Bilibili Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 16 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports wins 2 or more games than Bilibili Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming entered the LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 matchup against Top Esports as undefeated at 3-0 series (6-1 games), topping standings and fueling trader consensus as favorites in the double round-robin stage with Fearless Draft, where top 4 advance directly to playoffs. Top Esports sat close behind at 3-1 (6-2 games) on a hot streak, sweeping recent opponents 2-0 following JackeyLove's mid-split return from health issues that strengthened their botlane. Historically competitive head-to-head favored neither decisively, with patch 26.7 meta emphasizing macro execution amid high stakes for seeding; no reported injuries or roster changes altered lineups.

title: Military action against Iran ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate on a full calendar day by the listed date, Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. 

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate on a full calendar day by the listed date, Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. 

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends by

eventMetadata.context_description: US and Israeli offensive military operations against Iran, launched February 28 under Operation Epic Fury targeting nuclear sites, leadership, and missile capabilities, halted following a two-week ceasefire agreement announced April 7, with US officials confirming suspension of strikes while maintaining defensive actions and a naval blockade of Iranian ports. No verified airstrikes on Iranian soil have occurred since, per trader consensus reflecting the ceasefire's hold amid diplomatic efforts including Pakistan's mediation and talks in Islamabad. The truce nears expiration around April 21, with President Trump signaling the conflict is nearing resolution, though escalation risks persist if negotiations falter or Iran resumes proxy attacks via Hezbollah or militias.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 10 - April 17, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 200-219 tweets from April 10 to April 17, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 300-319 times on X from April 10 to April 17, 2026, at 54.5% implied probability, driven by his consistent 40-post daily average—reaching 279 tallied by the official xtracker as of late April 16—projecting a final tally near 312 amid steady activity. This edges out 320-339 (20.8%) and 280-299 (20.5%), reflecting minor variance in his high-volume social media rhythm fueled by viral Tesla Robotaxi updates, Cybercab hype, SpaceX megastructure reveals, and pointed commentary on justice reform. With the period concluding at noon ET April 17, traders eye last-hour posts for any momentum shift, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in tracking this cultural icon's unyielding X presence.

title: Baltimore Orioles vs. Cleveland Guardians
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Baltimore Orioles and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for April 16 at 6:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Baltimore Orioles" if the Baltimore Orioles win the game.

This market will resolve to "Cleveland Guardians" if the Cleveland Guardians win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if at least one run is scored in the 1st inning by either the Baltimore Orioles or Cleveland Guardians.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cleveland Guardians enjoy a home advantage at Progressive Field against the injury-plagued Baltimore Orioles, with both teams hovering around .500 early in the season—Orioles at 9-9 (3rd AL East), Guardians at 10-9 (2nd AL Central). Probable starters Chris Bassitt (0-2, 9.00 ERA) and Tanner Bibee (0-2, 6.38 ERA) have faltered, spotlighting bullpen depth amid Baltimore's recent rash of absences: Tyler O'Neill (7-day IL, concussion), Andrew Kittredge (15-day IL, shoulder), and Keegan Akin (10-day IL, groin). Gunnar Henderson's 6 HR lead Orioles' bats, but Guardians' 4-2 home mark and healthier roster fuel trader consensus on a competitive matchup under mild 58°F conditions.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the UEFA Europa League quarter-final second leg at the City Ground, Nottingham Forest's trader consensus at 100% implied probability for victory stems from their commanding 1-0 halftime lead via Morgan Gibbs-White's deflected strike, coupled with FC Porto's early red card to defender Jan Bednarek, leaving the visitors with 10 men and aggregate trailing 2-1. Forest's home advantage, solid defensive organization, and Porto's recent domestic wobbles—highlighted by a late Famalicao draw—have fueled this dominant positioning after a balanced 1-1 first leg. A Porto comeback would require at least two goals without reply amid numerical disadvantage, fatigue from travel, and Forest's clean sheet pursuit, though extra time or penalties remain theoretical upset paths.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori's commanding first-round lead in Peru's April 12-13 presidential election, with over 17% of votes amid 92% counted as of April 16, positions her as the trader-favored frontrunner at 65.5% implied probability for the June 7 runoff victory, bolstered by her Fuerza Popular party's right-wing base and past near-wins despite legal hurdles. Leftist congressman Roberto Sánchez Palomino surged to second at around 12% in late tallies from rural areas, displacing early leader Rafael López Aliaga and earning 21.7% odds as her probable runoff foe amid Peru's fragmented 35-candidate field. López Aliaga's fraud claims without evidence sustain his 11.5% share, but slow ONPE counts and JNE verification fuel uncertainty ahead of official second-place confirmation.

title: Porsche Tennis Grand Prix: Leylah Fernandez vs Zeynep Sonmez
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Leylah Fernandez and Zeynep Sonmez in the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix, originally scheduled for April 16, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Leylah Fernandez' if Leylah Fernandez advances against Zeynep Sonmez.

This market will resolve to 'Zeynep Sonmez' if Zeynep Sonmez advances against Leylah Fernandez.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Leylah Fernandez vs. Zeynep Sonmez: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Leylah Fernandez and Zeynep Sonmez in the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix, originally scheduled for April 16, 2026 at 2:00PM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."

eventMetadata.context_description: Zeynep Sonmez enters as the qualifier riding massive momentum after stunning fifth seed Jasmine Paolini 6-2, 6-2 in the round of 32 at the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix, her second top-10 upset this year following Beatriz Haddad Maia in Miami. Leylah Fernandez, ranked No. 25, advanced with a solid 6-1, 6-4 win over Alexandra Eala but carries a modest 5-9 record in 2026 amid inconsistent form. On indoor clay—Fernandez's stronger surface with a career quarterfinal at Roland Garros—their first head-to-head favors the Canadian's experience, though Sonmez's aggressive baseline play and recent confidence make this WTA 500 round-of-16 matchup closely contested. No injury concerns reported ahead of Thursday's clash.

title: Texas Rangers vs. Athletics
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Texas Rangers and Athletics, scheduled for April 16 at 3:05PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Texas Rangers" if the Texas Rangers win the game.

This market will resolve to "Athletics" if the Athletics win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the series finale at Sutter Health Park, Texas Rangers right-hander Jack Leiter (1-1, 4.9 ERA) faces Oakland Athletics lefty Jacob Lopez (1-1, 6.4 ERA), whose control issues have inflated his 7.4 WHIP. The Athletics grabbed a 6-5 win Tuesday via Shea Langeliers' and Jacob Wilson's home runs, extending their AL West lead atop a tight division, while Rangers dominated 8-1 Sunday behind Nathan Eovaldi's gem. Both teams hover near .500 (Rangers 9-9, Athletics 10-8), with Texas boosted by Wyatt Langford's return to the lineup post-quad tweak alongside Corey Seager's order flip. Rangers' superior staff ERA (3.25 vs. Athletics' 4.23) and deeper lineup counter Oakland's home advantage and recent momentum, though Texas' injury-thinned bullpen (Chris Martin shoulder) looms large.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and the final trading day during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil prices hover around $92 per barrel as of mid-April 2026, reflecting a pullback from early-month peaks near $104 amid Middle East geopolitical risks, including Iran conflict disruptions that slashed global supply by over 10 million b/d in March per IEA data. The EIA's latest weekly petroleum status report revealed a 0.9 million barrel crude inventory draw for the week ending April 10—surpassing expectations—coupled with a 9 million barrel total petroleum stock decline, supporting prices despite demand contraction forecasts of 80,000 b/d for 2026. OPEC+ plans incremental output hikes of 206,000 b/d starting May temper bullish pressures. Traders eye the April 22 EIA release and regional peace talks for shifts in the tight supply-demand balance.

title: Blues vs. Utah
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 16 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Blues win, the market will resolve to "Blues".
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

markets.question: Blues vs. Utah: O/U 7.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 16 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Blues and Utah combine to score 8 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 8, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis Blues head to Utah Mammoth for a late-season Central Division clash amid playoff positioning, with the Blues having clinched the second wild-card spot after a dominant 6-1 home win over Utah on April 15 that propelled their recent 6-3-1 form averaging 4.0 goals per game. Utah wraps a season-long seven-game homestand on the back of that loss, hampered by key absences—Jake McBain and Barrett Hayton out week-to-week with lower- and upper-body injuries, respectively, plus Sean Durzi day-to-day—while the Blues enter with a clean injury report. Home-ice advantage and goaltending matchup loom large in this evenly contested affair, where power-play efficiency and recent head-to-head dominance could sway trader consensus toward either side.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 17?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Real Betis Balompié vs. SC Braga
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Europa League game, scheduled for Thursday, April 16, 2026 between Real Betis Balompié and SC Braga.

markets.question: Will Real Betis Balompié vs. SC Braga end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 16, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: SC Braga's stunning comeback in the UEFA Europa League quarter-final second leg at Real Betis' Estadio Benito Villamarín propelled trader consensus to 100% on a Braga victory, following their 4-2 win on the night (5-3 aggregate after a 1-1 first leg). Betis surged to a 2-0 lead within 30 minutes via early goals, but Braga, led by Ricardo Horta's penalty and Pau Víctor's brace, overturned the deficit with relentless attacking pressure and clinical finishing, exploiting Betis' defensive lapses despite home advantage. This dominant performance reflects Braga's knockout pedigree and momentum into the semi-finals. With the official UEFA result confirmed, no realistic challenges remain—upsets or red cards are moot post-whistle.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich's dramatic 4-3 second-leg victory over Real Madrid (6-4 aggregate) on April 15 propelled them into the UEFA Champions League semifinals against PSG, who dominated Liverpool 4-0 on aggregate, fueling trader consensus with Bayern at 34.5% implied probability as slight favorites amid their strong league-phase form and late-game resilience from Luis Díaz and Michael Olise. Arsenal advanced 1-0 aggregate via a tense 0-0 home draw against Sporting CP, while Atlético Madrid edged Barcelona 3-2 aggregate, keeping the race tight at 27.5% and 25.5% for Arsenal and PSG, respectively, with Atlético at 11.5% reflecting their defensive grit in knockouts. These grueling quarterfinals highlight the competitive semifinal path, where home advantage in first legs (late April) and head-to-head histories could shift dynamics.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 72.5% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizing by April 30—defined as IMF PortWatch's 7-day moving average of transits exceeding 60—as recent Kpler and MarineTraffic data confirm volumes lingering below 10% of pre-February crisis norms amid the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports, enforced since April 10. Primarily Iran-linked vessels account for the trickle of activity, with Pentagon reports of 13 turnbacks in the past day underscoring ongoing enforcement and risks from suspected sea mines. This sustains 20% global oil supply disruptions, elevating crude prices and freight rates 30–50%, while de-escalation announcements or full strait access could catalyze a sentiment shift ahead of resolution.

title: Ducks vs. Predators
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 16 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Ducks win, the market will resolve to "Ducks".
If the Predators win, the market will resolve to "Predators".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anaheim Ducks visit Nashville Predators for a season finale at Bridgestone Arena amid Ducks' defensive injury crisis, including Radko Gudas (lower-body), Pavel Mintyukov (lower-body), and illness sidelining Chris Kreider, forcing lineup tweaks like Lukas Dostal in net. Ducks (42-33-6, Pacific contenders) enter slumping 1-6-2 in nine games since late March, capped by a 5-0 shutout loss to Preds on April 7 where Justus Annunen stopped 43 shots. Playoff-eliminated Predators (38-33-10, Central) hold home-ice advantage and recent head-to-head edge despite day-to-day status for Roman Josi and Nic Hague (upper-body), with mixed form including losses to Sharks and Mammoth but wins fueled by Stamkos' 40th goal. Trader consensus reflects Ducks' talent versus momentum deficit and rest factors heading into playoffs.

title: Tampa Bay Rays vs. Chicago White Sox
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Tampa Bay Rays and Chicago White Sox, scheduled for April 16 at 2:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Tampa Bay Rays" if the Tampa Bay Rays win the game.

This market will resolve to "Chicago White Sox" if the Chicago White Sox win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Rays hold a commanding edge entering Thursday's series finale at Rate Field, riding a five-game win streak after sweeping the first three games versus the Chicago White Sox 8-5, 8-3, and 5-3, improving to AL East leaders around 13-7 while Sox sink to 6-15 in AL Central basement. Shane McClanahan's recent return anchored early victories amid White Sox pitching struggles, now exacerbated by IL stints for catcher Kyle Teel (hamstring) and RHP Jonathan Cannon (hip). Probable matchup pits Rays LHP Steven Matz (3-0, 3.94 ERA) against Sox RHP Jordan Leasure (1-0, 4.00 ERA), with both bullpens depleted—Rays missing relievers Edwin Uceta and Garrett Cleavinger (shoulders). Mild 69°F conditions with 13 mph right-to-left winds could boost offense.

title: 2026 Men's French Open Winner
description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's straight-sets victory over Carlos Alcaraz in the Monte Carlo Masters final last weekend—his maiden ATP Masters 1000 clay title and reclaiming the world No. 1 ranking—has propelled him to a slim lead in trader consensus for the 2026 Roland Garros crown, underscoring his surging clay-court form and fresh head-to-head edge. Alcaraz remains a razor-close challenger at 36%, buoyed by his proven Grand Slam pedigree on the surface, multiple French Open deep runs, and explosive baseline game suited to slower conditions. With Madrid underway and Rome looming in the clay swing, the tightly bunched top duo reflects balanced expectations amid no major injury concerns, while Zverev trails on consistent quarterfinal-or-better clay history but lacks recent breakthroughs.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid escalating US-Iran tensions in the 2026 conflict, Kharg Island—Iran's critical Persian Gulf oil export terminal handling 90% of its crude—remains under full Iranian control following US and Israeli airstrikes on military targets there on April 7. Iranian state media, including Mehr News Agency, released footage affirming the island's security, ongoing armed forces presence, and uninterrupted oil exports, countering US claims of destruction. President Trump's repeated threats of seizure to cripple Iran's economy have fueled speculation, but no ground operations or territorial losses have materialized, with Pentagon assessments highlighting operational risks. Traders monitor potential further airstrikes, Strait of Hormuz blockades, or diplomatic breakthroughs that could shift control dynamics before market resolution dates.
